## [00:00 - 03:02]

Hey guys, what's up? In this video we're going to talk about tags. Tags next to custom fields are probably your best friends inside your messenger flows because tags enable you to track how people use your bot which actions they take. You can use tags to create filters, you can use tags to create website custom audiences, you can use tags to exclude people from your broadcasts, they're awesome but they can also get really messy quick and that's why I built a solution for you guys so you can create proper naming conventions for your tags so you are able to find them easily when you want to use them in different scenarios when you build your flows and your sequences and whatever. So what I'm going to do is in this video I'm going to show you how you can use tags in your flows to segment people. I'm going to show you how you can use rules with tags to trigger or not trigger a specific action. I'm going to show you how you can use tags to create filters in your audiences and last but not least I'm going to show you the dynamic messenger tagging framework we built for you and which you can download and use for your own bots. So I'm going to share my screen and I'm going to dive right in. There we go. In this example I'm going to show you how we use tags in a lead qualifying flow. So what I said already was we can use tags to segment people. We can apply tags to people who took specific actions which then enables us to filter on okay if this person has this tag then we know he did that. Now take a bit of the stuff further if he has this tag but he doesn't have that tag we know that they didn't take the first the next step and that is valuable information because sometimes we want to have people take the next step but they didn't for some reason so we can create a filter if they have this tag but they don't have that tag then I want to segment that audience and I want to send that audience specific message. Tags again I'm going to say it again tags are your best friends. Tag as much as possible but the issue with that can be is that tagging can become messy and when tagging becomes messy it will not work as you wanted to work because then you're going to create different tags for the same purpose and then you're going to filter on one tag but that doesn't include all the audiences you got you got you got a different meaning convention for another tag does the same thing and mess so bear with me I'm going to show you exactly how to solve their problem.
